message:
feat(simulation): add sandboxed skill execution environment

Implements a comprehensive simulation sandbox for testing skill execution
in isolated environments with configurable safety constraints, timeout
handling, and detailed execution tracking.

New file src/simulation.rs (769 lines):

SimulationConfig struct:
- allow_network: bool - Block/allow network access during execution
- allow_external_fs: bool - Restrict filesystem access to workspace
- command_timeout: Duration - Per-command timeout (default 30s)
- total_timeout: Duration - Total simulation timeout (default 5m)
- blocked_commands: Vec<String> - Forbidden command patterns
- capture_output: bool - Record stdout/stderr
- track_file_changes: bool - Monitor filesystem modifications

SimulationEngine:
- new(ctx, config): Initialize with app context and configuration
- run(skill, input): Execute skill in sandbox, return SimulationResult
- validate_command(): Check command against blocked patterns
- check_network_access(): Detect network-requiring operations
- check_fs_access(): Validate path is within allowed boundaries
- execute_step(): Run individual step with timeout enforcement

SimulationResult struct:
- success: bool - Overall execution success
- steps: Vec<StepResult> - Individual step outcomes
- file_changes: Vec<FileChange> - Tracked filesystem modifications
- duration: Duration - Total execution time
- error: Option<String> - Error message if failed
- warnings: Vec<String> - Non-fatal issues encountered

Safety features:
- DCG (Danger Check Guard) integration for command classification
- Path traversal prevention with symlink resolution
- Environment variable sanitization
- Process isolation via subprocess management
- Automatic cleanup of temporary artifacts

New file src/cli/commands/simulate.rs:
- `simulate run <skill>`: Execute skill in sandbox
- `simulate dry-run <skill>`: Validate without execution
- --timeout, --no-network, --no-external-fs flags
- --blocked-commands for additional restrictions
- JSON output mode for CI/CD integration

Module registration:
- src/lib.rs: Export simulation module
- src/cli/commands/mod.rs: Register simulate command

Use cases:
- Test untrusted skills before adding to library
- Validate skill behavior in CI pipelines
- Debug skill execution with detailed tracing
- Demonstrate skill capabilities safely to users

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
